title: Unit Final: Foundations of Digital Citizenship

questions.question: Which statement best defines digital citizenship?
options: Responsible, ethical, and safe use of technology
The ability to access the internet anywhere
questions.correct_answer: Responsible, ethical, and safe use of technology

feedback.Using technology only for entertainment: This is too limited. Digital citizenship involves more than entertainment use.
feedback.Responsible, ethical, and safe use of te: Correct. Digital citizenship includes behavior, responsibility, and safety online.
feedback.Owning multiple digital devices: Ownership does not define digital citizenship.
feedback.The ability to access the internet anywh: Access is part of digital citizenship, but not the full definition.

questions.question: Digital citizenship only applies when you are using social media platforms.

feedback.True: Incorrect. Digital citizenship applies to all online and digital interactions.
feedback.False: Correct. Digital citizenship applies across all digital environments.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of digital inclusion?
options: Using only one device for all users
Ensuring everyone has equal access to digital tools and resources
Limiting internet access to certain groups
questions.correct_answer: Ensuring everyone has equal access to digital tools and resources

feedback.Blocking access to educational websites: This restricts access rather than promotes inclusion.
feedback.Using only one device for all users: This does not ensure equal or effective access.
feedback.Ensuring everyone has equal access to di: Correct. Inclusion focuses on equitable access.

questions.question: A student excludes a classmate from an online group project chat. What concept does this most directly relate to?

feedback.Digital security: Security involves protecting information, not participation.
feedback.Digital access and inclusion: Correct. Excluding someone limits their participation and access.
feedback.Digital footprint: This refers to online presence, not access.
feedback.Digital etiquette: Etiquette involves behavior, but this scenario focuses more on inclusion.

questions.question: Using respectful language online is part of digital etiquette.

feedback.False: Incorrect. Respectful language is essential to proper online behavior.
feedback.True: Correct. Respectful communication is a key part of digital etiquette.

questions.question: Which response demonstrates appropriate digital etiquette?
options: Responding respectfully even when you disagree
Posting a rude comment when you disagree
questions.correct_answer: Responding respectfully even when you disagree

feedback.Ignoring all online communication: This does not demonstrate engagement or etiquette.

questions.question: A student receives a mean comment online and feels upset. What is the most responsible first step?
options: Pause and decide whether to report or block the user
Respond with an equally mean comment
Share the comment publicly to embarrass the sender
questions.correct_answer: Pause and decide whether to report or block the user

feedback.Respond with an equally mean comment: Escalating conflict is not responsible behavior.
feedback.Share the comment publicly to embarrass: This escalates the situation and is not appropriate.
feedback.Ignore it and do nothing: Ignoring may not always be the best solution.

questions.question: Thinking before posting online can help prevent digital conflicts.

feedback.False: Incorrect. Thinking ahead is key to responsible digital behavior.
feedback.True: Correct. Thoughtful posting reduces misunderstandings and conflict.

questions.question: Which scenario best demonstrates responsible digital decision-making?
options: Forwarding a message without verifying it
Taking time to evaluate the impact before sharing content
Reacting immediately to every online message
questions.correct_answer: Taking time to evaluate the impact before sharing content

feedback.Taking time to evaluate the impact befor: Correct. Responsible decisions require careful thinking.
feedback.Reacting immediately to every online mes: Impulsive reactions can lead to problems.

questions.question: During an online discussion, two students strongly disagree. What is the best approach to manage the conflict?
options: Respectfully explain your perspective and listen to theirs
Insult the other person to prove your point
questions.correct_answer: Respectfully explain your perspective and listen to theirs

feedback.Stop participating completely: Avoiding the situation may not resolve the issue.
feedback.Respectfully explain your perspective an: Correct. This promotes constructive conflict resolution.
feedback.Report them immediately without interact: Reporting is appropriate only if behavior violates guidelines.
feedback.Insult the other person to prove your po: This escalates conflict and is inappropriate.
